description: Configuration file for deploying OpenClaw AI agents to Kubernetes.
title: Shadow Cloud Configuration

name.description: Human-readable name for this deployment config (shown in console)

description.description: Description of what this agent team does

locale.description: Active locale for i18n resolution (e.g. "en", "zh-CN"). Defaults to "en".

i18n.description:
Internationalization dictionary.
Keyed by locale → key → translated string.

Template strings can reference translations via `${i18n:key}`.
The active locale is determined by `config.locale`.

team.description:
Team / agent pack definition.
Groups agents with shared defaults.

use.description:
Global plugin declarations — webpack-style "use" pattern.
Each entry specifies a plugin id and optional configuration.

plugins.description:
Legacy plugin declarations map.
Prefer `use`, but keep this field for compatibility during migration.

registry.description:
Reusable provider/configuration registry.
Also contains vault definitions (registry.vaults) for secret isolation.

TeamConfig.description:
Team / agent pack metadata — groups agents into a cohesive named team.
Inspired by CrewClaw's "Agent Packs" concept.

description.description: What this team does — appears in console and CLI output

defaultModel.description: Default model applied to agents that don't set `agent.model`

defaultCompliance.description: Default compliance policy applied to agents that don't set `agent.compliance`

AgentModel.description:
Agent model configuration — mirrors gitagent's model section.
Specifies primary model with ordered fallbacks and inference constraints.

fallbacks.description: Ordered fallback models tried if primary is unavailable

allOf.description:
Inclusive minimum value constraint (value >= min).

`Minimum<N>` is a type tag that validates numeric values are greater than or
equal to the specified bound. Apply it to `number` or `bigint` properties
using TypeScript intersection types.

This constraint is **mutually exclusive** with {@link ExclusiveMinimum} - you
cannot use both on the same property. Use `Minimum` for inclusive bounds (>=)
and `ExclusiveMinimum` for exclusive bounds (>).

The constraint is enforced at runtime by `typia.is()`, `typia.assert()`, and
`typia.validate()`. It also generates `minimum` in JSON Schema output.

typia.tag.description:
Compile-time marker property for typia transformer.

This phantom property carries tag metadata in the type system. It is never
assigned at runtime - it exists only for the transformer to read during
compilation.

allOf.description:
Inclusive maximum value constraint (value <= max).

`Maximum<N>` is a type tag that validates numeric values are less than or
equal to the specified bound. Apply it to `number` or `bigint` properties
using TypeScript intersection types.

This constraint is **mutually exclusive** with {@link ExclusiveMaximum} - you
cannot use both on the same property. Use `Maximum` for inclusive bounds (<=)
and `ExclusiveMaximum` for exclusive bounds (<).

The constraint is enforced at runtime by `typia.is()`, `typia.assert()`, and
`typia.validate()`. It also generates `maximum` in JSON Schema output.

typia.tag.description:
Compile-time marker property for typia transformer.

This phantom property carries tag metadata in the type system. It is never
assigned at runtime - it exists only for the transformer to read during
compilation.

allOf.description:
Numeric precision and bit-width type constraint.

`Type<Value>` is a type tag that constrains numeric values to specific
bit-width representations. This is essential for Protocol Buffers
serialization and ensures values fit within their specified ranges.

Available types:

- `"int32"`: Signed 32-bit integer (-2,147,483,648 to 2,147,483,647)
- `"uint32"`: Unsigned 32-bit integer (0 to 4,294,967,295)
- `"int64"`: Signed 64-bit integer (for `number` or `bigint`)
- `"uint64"`: Unsigned 64-bit integer (for `number` or `bigint`)
- `"float"`: 32-bit floating point
- `"double"`: 64-bit floating point (default JavaScript number)

For Protocol Buffers, integer types also determine the wire encoding. The
constraint is enforced at runtime by `typia.is()`, `typia.assert()`, and
`typia.validate()`. It generates appropriate `type` in JSON Schema.

typia.tag.description:
Compile-time marker property for typia transformer.

This phantom property carries tag metadata in the type system. It is never
assigned at runtime - it exists only for the transformer to read during
compilation.

allOf.description:

`Minimum<N>` is a type tag that validates numeric values are greater than or
equal to the specified bound. Apply it to `number` or `bigint` properties
using TypeScript intersection types.

This constraint is **mutually exclusive** with {@link ExclusiveMinimum} - you
cannot use both on the same property. Use `Minimum` for inclusive bounds (>=)
and `ExclusiveMinimum` for exclusive bounds (>).

The constraint is enforced at runtime by `typia.is()`, `typia.assert()`, and
`typia.validate()`. It also generates `minimum` in JSON Schema output.

typia.tag.description:

This phantom property carries tag metadata in the type system. It is never
assigned at runtime - it exists only for the transformer to read during
compilation.

The constraint is enforced at runtime by `typia.is()`, `typia.assert()`, and

AgentCompliance.description:
Agent compliance/risk policy.
Mirrors gitagent compliance fields in camelCase for internal usage.

riskTier.description: Risk tier for this agent's tasks

frameworks.description: Regulatory / governance frameworks to align with

UseEntry.description: A single plugin declaration in the `use` array.

RegistryConfig.description: Registry section — reusable model provider, configuration presets, and vault definitions.

vaults.description:
Vault definitions for secret isolation.
Each agent references a vault by name (default: "default").
Per-agent K8s Secrets are generated with only the relevant keys.

request.description: Transport overrides (e.g. allowPrivateNetwork for proxy environments)

extends.description:
Base configuration to inherit from.
Resolved recursively — child fields override parent.

model.description:
Model preset for agents using this configuration.
After extends resolution, merged into AgentConfiguration.model.
Priority: agent.model > configuration.model > team.defaultModel.

allOf.description:
Numeric precision and bit-width type constraint.

`Type<Value>` is a type tag that constrains numeric values to specific
bit-width representations. This is essential for Protocol Buffers
serialization and ensures values fit within their specified ranges.

Available types:

- `"int32"`: Signed 32-bit integer (-2,147,483,648 to 2,147,483,647)
- `"uint32"`: Unsigned 32-bit integer (0 to 4,294,967,295)
- `"int64"`: Signed 64-bit integer (for `number` or `bigint`)
- `"uint64"`: Unsigned 64-bit integer (for `number` or `bigint`)
- `"float"`: 32-bit floating point
- `"double"`: 64-bit floating point (default JavaScript number)

For Protocol Buffers, integer types also determine the wire encoding. The
constraint is enforced at runtime by `typia.is()`, `typia.assert()`, and
`typia.validate()`. It generates appropriate `type` in JSON Schema.

default.description: Whether this is the default agent

params.description: Per-agent params merged over model catalog

acp.description: OpenClaw ACP runtime config for an agent.

instructions.description: Final instructions text (mapped from personality + systemPrompt)

OpenClawAcpRuntime.description: OpenClaw ACP runtime config for an agent.

OpenClawBinding.description: OpenClaw binding entry for multi-agent routing.

OpenClawChannelsConfig.description: OpenClaw channels section — each channel key matches official docs.

configuration.description: Agent configuration (can extend a base config)

identity.description:
Agent identity / soul — who this agent is.
systemPrompt and personality are merged into the OpenClaw system prompt.
Equivalent to SOUL.md in the gitagent standard.

model.description:
Model preferences — overrides registry and team defaults.
Mirrors gitagent's `model` section: preferred primary + ordered fallbacks.

compliance.description:
Compliance/risk policy for this agent.
Falls back to team.defaultCompliance when omitted.

workflows.description:
Deterministic multi-step workflows this agent can run.
Mirrors gitagent's SkillsFlow workflows/*.yaml.

use.description:
Per-agent plugin declarations (webpack-style "use" pattern).
Each entry specifies a plugin and its options.

description.description: Short description shown in console and CLI output

source.description:
Agent source overlay — pulls files from a git repository or local path
into the container using the gitagent directory convention.

When set, the agent container will have SOUL.md, RULES.md, skills/,
tools/, hooks/, etc. available at `source.mountPath` (default: /agent).
OpenClaw's agentDir is automatically configured to read from this path.

Supports two strategies:
- "init-container": Runtime git clone via K8s init container (default)
- "build-image":    Bake files into Docker image layer at build time

vault.description:
Vault reference — which vault to use for this agent's secrets.
Defaults to "default". Each vault generates an isolated K8s Secret.

permissions.description:
Per-tool permission policy.
Controls which tools can auto-execute vs. require human approval.
Maps to ACPX permission modes in the generated OpenClaw config.

networking.description:
Network egress policy for this agent's pods.
Generates a K8s NetworkPolicy resource.

version.description:
Agent configuration version (semver).
Recorded in K8s Deployment annotations for rollback tracking.

changelog.description: Change description for this version (stored in K8s annotations)

AgentConfiguration.description: Agent-level configuration that can extend a base config.

extends.description: Base configuration ID to extend from registry.configurations

model.description:
Model preferences resolved from registry.configurations preset.
Populated after extends resolution; lower priority than agent.model.

AgentIdentity.description:
Agent identity / soul — who the agent is.
Mirrors gitagent's SOUL.md concept, expressed inline in the config.

description.description: One-line description of what this agent does

personality.description:
Personality, communication style, values, domain expertise.
Free-form text injected before the main system prompt.
Equivalent to SOUL.md content.

systemPrompt.description:
Full system prompt override. When set, replaces any inherited prompt.
If `personality` is also set, it is prepended to this value.

**Priority** (highest to lowest):
1. `identity.systemPrompt` — overrides everything
2. `configuration.openclaw.agents[].systemPrompt` — OpenClaw agent level
3. `OpenClawAgentConfig.systemPrompt` — convenience field in config preset

When `identity.personality` is also set, final prompt is:
`${personality}\n\n${systemPrompt}`

AgentWorkflowDef.description:
Deterministic multi-step workflow — mirrors gitagent's SkillsFlow / workflows/*.yaml.
Chains skills, agents, and tools with explicit dependencies and data flow.

triggers.description: Events or cron expressions that trigger this workflow

steps.description: Ordered/parallel steps keyed by step ID

allOf.description:
Numeric precision and bit-width type constraint.

`Type<Value>` is a type tag that constrains numeric values to specific
bit-width representations. This is essential for Protocol Buffers
serialization and ensures values fit within their specified ranges.

Available types:

- `"uint32"`: Unsigned 32-bit integer (0 to 4,294,967,295)
- `"int64"`: Signed 64-bit integer (for `number` or `bigint`)
- `"uint64"`: Unsigned 64-bit integer (for `number` or `bigint`)
- `"float"`: 32-bit floating point
- `"double"`: 64-bit floating point (default JavaScript number)

For Protocol Buffers, integer types also determine the wire encoding. The
constraint is enforced at runtime by `typia.is()`, `typia.assert()`, and

typia.tag.description:
Compile-time marker property for typia transformer.

This phantom property carries tag metadata in the type system. It is never
assigned at runtime - it exists only for the transformer to read during
compilation.

AgentSource.description:
Agent file source specification.
Points the agent at a git repository (or local path) that follows the
gitagent directory layout: SOUL.md, RULES.md, agent.yaml, skills/, tools/,
hooks/, skillflows/, memory/, compliance/, etc.

path.description:
Local filesystem path (dev/CI mode).
Resolved at `shadowob-cloud up` time and baked into a ConfigMap or
used for local adapter pre-population.

strategy.description:
Strategy for delivering source files into the container.
Default: "init-container"

mountPath.description:
Path inside the container where the agent source is mounted.
OpenClaw reads SOUL.md, RULES.md, skills/, etc. from this directory.
Default: "/agent"

include.description:
Specific file/directory patterns to copy from the source.
Default: all standard gitagent files (SOUL.md, RULES.md, skills/, etc.)
Use this to limit what gets overlaid.

gitagent.description:
When true, the mounted directory is treated as a gitagent standard layout.
The adapter will:
  - set OpenClaw's agentDir to mountPath (reads SOUL.md, RULES.md automatically)
  - parse agent.yaml model config and merge into agent.model
  - parse hooks/hooks.yaml and map to OpenClaw hooks
  - enumerate skillflows/*.yaml and map to agent.workflows
  - configure compliance from compliance/regulatory-map.yaml
Default: true

imageTag.description:
When strategy is "build-image": Docker registry to push the built image to.
e.g. "ghcr.io/my-org/my-agent:latest"
If omitted, uses a local image name based on agentId.

GitSource.description: Git repository reference for an agent source.

ref.description: Branch, tag, or full SHA. Defaults to "main"

dir.description:
Subdirectory inside the repository that contains the agent definition
(e.g. "agents/my-agent" in a monorepo). Defaults to repo root.

allOf.description:
Numeric precision and bit-width type constraint.

`Type<Value>` is a type tag that constrains numeric values to specific
bit-width representations. This is essential for Protocol Buffers
serialization and ensures values fit within their specified ranges.

Available types:

- `"int32"`: Signed 32-bit integer (-2,147,483,648 to 2,147,483,647)
- `"uint32"`: Unsigned 32-bit integer (0 to 4,294,967,295)
- `"int64"`: Signed 64-bit integer (for `number` or `bigint`)
- `"uint64"`: Unsigned 64-bit integer (for `number` or `bigint`)
- `"float"`: 32-bit floating point
- `"double"`: 64-bit floating point (default JavaScript number)

For Protocol Buffers, integer types also determine the wire encoding. The
constraint is enforced at runtime by `typia.is()`, `typia.assert()`, and
`typia.validate()`. It generates appropriate `type` in JSON Schema.

typia.tag.description:
Compile-time marker property for typia transformer.

This phantom property carries tag metadata in the type system. It is never
assigned at runtime - it exists only for the transformer to read during
compilation.

sshKeySecret.description:
Name of a K8s Secret that contains an SSH private key for private repos.
The secret must have a key named `ssh-privatekey`.

tokenSecret.description:
Name of a K8s Secret (or literal token via ${env:VAR}) for HTTPS auth.
Used as: git clone https://<token>@host/repo

AgentPermissions.description:
Per-tool permission policy for an agent.
Inspired by Claude Managed Agents' toolset-level permission control.

default.description: Default permission level for all tools

tools.description: Per-tool overrides — key is tool name, supports glob patterns (e.g. "mcp-*")

nonInteractive.description: Behaviour when the agent runs non-interactively (no human in the loop)

PermissionLevel.description: Permission level for agent tools — maps to ACPX permission modes.

AgentNetworking.description:
Per-agent networking policy configuration.
Generates K8s NetworkPolicy resources to control egress.

allowedHosts.description: Allowed egress domains (requires Cilium for FQDN-level enforcement)

allowMcpServers.description: Allow egress to MCP server endpoints (auto-extracted from agent config)

allowPackageManagers.description: Allow egress to package manager registries (npm, pypi, etc.)

SharedWorkspaceConfig.description:
Shared workspace configuration — creates a PersistentVolumeClaim
that is mounted into every agent container, so agents share a
distributed filesystem that OpenClaw can discover.

storageClassName.description: Storage class name (empty for cluster default)

CloudSkillsConfig.description:
Cloud-level skills configuration — manages skill installation
and distribution across agent containers.

installDir.description: Directory inside the container where skills are installed

CloudSkillEntry.description:
Cloud-level skill entry — specifies a skill to load
from a registry or directory into the agent containers.

name.description: Skill identifier (npm package name or local directory name)

source.description: Skill source: "bundled" (built-in), "npm" (install from registry), or "path" (local directory)

version.description: For npm: package version. For path: directory on the host.

path.description: For path source: the local directory path
